explanationZhTw: 「reintegration」指「重新融入、再整合」；在這裡是說把一個人重新帶回原本的秩序或體系中。
generatedExample: After months abroad, his reintegration into the team took some time.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「acceptance」不是單純的「接受某物」，而是指心理上認清並接受現實狀況。
generatedExample: Acceptance of the new rules came slowly.

explanationZhTw: 「enter upon」是較正式的說法，意思是「開始、著手進入」某個階段或過程。
generatedExample: She is ready to enter upon a new chapter of her life.

explanationZhTw: 「最近／近來」的意思，較書面或文學用法。
generatedExample: Of late, she has been working from home more often.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「債券」；而是指束縛身體的繩索或拘束物。
generatedExample: The prisoner struggled against the bonds that tied him to the chair.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「恐怖行為」；而是指令人害怕、很可怕的東西。
generatedExample: At first the exam was a terror, but later it felt manageable.

explanationZhTw: 「躲避、避開」，常指巧妙地避開問題、追問或傷害。
generatedExample: She tried to evade the question by changing the topic.

explanationZhTw: 指突發的劇痛或一陣強烈不適；在這裡是「痛楚」。
generatedExample: He felt pangs of pain after the surgery.

explanationZhTw: 形容反應快、機靈、腦筋轉得快。
generatedExample: A quick-witted student answered the tricky question immediately.

explanationZhTw: 「主要地、尤其是」，比 everyday 用法更正式。
generatedExample: The damage was chiefly caused by heavy rain.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「熬過、撐完、順利完成」。
generatedExample: We got through the meeting faster than expected.

explanationZhTw: 指一段正式活動、治療、會議或使用某設備的時間。
generatedExample: The tutor held a short practice session after class.

explanationZhTw: 表示「不明確的、無法確定長短的」。
generatedExample: The project was delayed for an indefinite period.

explanationZhTw: 指兩個事件之間的時間間隔。
generatedExample: There were long intervals between the train arrivals.

explanationZhTw: 「expend」在這裡不是單純的「花掉」，而是較正式地表示「耗費／花費（時間、金錢、精力等資源）」。
generatedExample: The team expended a lot of time fixing a problem that should have been caught earlier.

explanationZhTw: 「puzzled」表示「感到困惑、摸不著頭緒」，比一般的「confused」更偏向「不知道原因或答案」。
generatedExample: She was puzzled by the strange message and asked for help.

explanationZhTw: 「grasp」在這裡是「理解、掌握」的意思，不是字面上的「抓住」。
generatedExample: He can grasp the main idea quickly, but he still needs practice with details.

explanationZhTw: 「mechanics」在此不是機械零件，而是指「運作方式、內部機制」。
generatedExample: I understand the mechanics of the app, but not why users prefer it.

explanationZhTw: 「sanity」指「理智、精神正常狀態」。這裡是在說他開始懷疑自己的理智是否正常。
generatedExample: After three sleepless nights, he started to doubt his own sanity.

explanationZhTw: 「collaborated」表示「合作、共同參與」。這裡不是單純的「一起做」，而是指「我有參與共同寫作」；在文學或工作語境裡很常見。
generatedExample: She collaborated with two editors on the final draft.

explanationZhTw: 「produced」在這裡是「製作、完成、寫成」的意思，不只是一般的「生產」。在被動語態中，它強調一本書不是由單一個人獨立完成的。
generatedExample: The report was produced by a small research team.

explanationZhTw: 「individually」表示「單獨地、各自地」。這裡用來強調書本通常不是由某一個人完全獨立完成，而是需要合作。
generatedExample: Each form must be completed individually.

explanationZhTw: 「true」表示「真的、屬實的」。這裡是在問某件事是否屬實。
generatedExample: Is it true that the report was published yesterday?

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「計畫、方案」；不是電視節目，而是某種政治或思想上的整體主張。
generatedExample: The new programme includes tax cuts and school reform.

explanationZhTw: 正式用法，意思是「提出、陳述、列出」某個計畫或論點。
generatedExample: The document sets forth three clear goals for the project.

explanationZhTw: 指「累積、積聚」；這裡是知識的逐步累積。
generatedExample: The accumulation of evidence changed the case.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「啟發」，而是偏思想、政治語境中的「啟蒙、覺醒」。
generatedExample: The book helped bring enlightenment to many readers.

explanationZhTw: 指「無產階級的、工人階級的」；屬於政治與社會階級詞彙。
generatedExample: The film portrays a proletarian family in the city.

explanationZhTw: 指「反叛、起義、叛亂」；比一般的反對更強烈。
generatedExample: The rebellion spread from the capital to nearby towns.

explanationZhTw: 是 foresee 的過去式，意思是「預見到、事先想到」。
generatedExample: She foresaw that the meeting would run late.

explanationZhTw: 指「起義、反抗、暴動」；這裡是群眾對政權發動反抗。
generatedExample: The workers revolted after months of unfair treatment.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「一直珍視、懷抱著」的意思，帶有強烈感情色彩。
generatedExample: He cherished the hope of seeing his hometown again.

explanationZhTw: 指「暴動、武裝叛亂、起義」；比 rebellion 更正式、更偏政治軍事語境。
generatedExample: The army was sent in to stop the insurrection.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「放棄」某個想法或計畫。
generatedExample: We had to abandon the plan because of the storm.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是政治語境中的「被推翻、被顛覆」。
generatedExample: The old regime was overthrown after the election.

explanationZhTw: 指「起點、出發點」；這裡是思考的基礎前提。
generatedExample: Customer needs should be the starting-point of any design.

explanationZhTw: 「get back to」在這裡是「回到、重新談論」的意思，不是字面上的「回到某處」。
generatedExample: Let's get back to the main issue after lunch.

explanationZhTw: 「in power」指「掌權、執政中」，是政治語境裡的慣用說法。
generatedExample: The same party has been in power for ten years.

explanationZhTw: 「cling to power」是慣用語，表示「死抓著權力不放、千方百計保住權位」。
generatedExample: Some leaders cling to power even when people want change.

explanationZhTw: 「go on」在這裡是命令語氣，意思是「繼續說、接著講」。
generatedExample: Go on, I'm listening.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「壓垮、淹沒」。在這裡表示疲倦感強到幾乎把他整個人蓋住。
generatedExample: After the long journey, she felt overwhelmed by exhaustion.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 bulk 不是「體積」，而是「大部分、絕大多數」；整個片語表示「絕大多數的人」。
generatedExample: The great bulk of the work was finished before lunch.

explanationZhTw: barbarities 指「殘暴行為、野蠻暴行」；這裡強調政權對人民使用的殘酷手段。
generatedExample: The report described the barbarities committed during the war.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，意思是「公正而認真地聽取」。這裡指對方願意把你的論點完整聽完，而不是草率否定。
generatedExample: Before we criticize the plan, we should give it a fair hearing.

explanationZhTw: lunacy 在這裡是「瘋狂、極不理性」的意思，不是字面診斷，而是批評對方固執到荒謬。
generatedExample: It would be lunacy to ignore such a clear warning.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「統治、支配」；不是單純的「管理」，而是帶有掌控他人命運的政治意味。
generatedExample: The council has been ruling over the region for decades.

explanationZhTw: 這是常見說法，意思是「為了我們自己的利益／好處」；常帶有一點替別人做主、但自認是善意的語氣。
generatedExample: My parents took away my phone for my own good.

explanationZhTw: `fit to` 在這裡不是「適合穿戴」那種意思，而是「有能力／有資格」；整句是在說「不適合自己治理自己」。
generatedExample: He is not fit to lead a team yet.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `started` 不是「開始」，而是指「突然一驚、猛地一震」。屬於情境上較特殊的用法。
generatedExample: She started when the phone rang behind her.

explanationZhTw: `cry out` 表示「大叫、驚呼」。在這裡是因為疼痛或驚嚇而差點叫出聲。
generatedExample: He cried out when he touched the hot pan.

explanationZhTw: `pang` 是「一陣劇痛、突然的刺痛感」，常用來描述短暫但強烈的身體或情緒感受。
generatedExample: She felt a pang of sadness when she saw the old photo.

explanationZhTw: `shot through` 在這裡不是字面上的「射穿」，而是指疼痛「迅速竄過、猛然傳遍」身體。這是很典型的情境化用法。
generatedExample: A sharp pain shot through his leg as he stood up.

explanationZhTw: `lever` 是「槓桿、拉桿、操作桿」。這裡指可以被推動來調整數值的控制桿。
generatedExample: He pulled the lever to open the gate.

explanationZhTw: 「stupid」意為「愚蠢的、笨的」。在這裡是直接責備 Winston 的行為不明智，語氣很強烈。
generatedExample: It was stupid to leave the door unlocked.

explanationZhTw: 「stupid」意為「愚蠢的、笨的」。在這裡是直接責備 Winston 的行為不明智，語氣很強烈。
generatedExample: It was stupid to leave the door unlocked.

explanationZhTw: 「know better than to...」是常見說法，意思是「應該知道不該做某事／說某事」。帶有責備意味，表示對方本來就該懂得避免這種行為。
generatedExample: You should know better than to interrupt the teacher.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示做某事是為了那件事本身，而不是為了別的目的。
generatedExample: She studies music for its own sake, not to impress anyone.

explanationZhTw: 副詞，意思是「僅僅、完全只」。這裡強調目標只有權力。
generatedExample: The service is solely for members of the club.

explanationZhTw: 專門用語，指由少數人掌權的統治集團或寡頭政治體制。
generatedExample: The country was ruled by oligarchies for many years.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「在做法上非常接近」，不是字面上的靠近距離。
generatedExample: Her plan came very close to our original idea.

explanationZhTw: 動詞，意思是「假裝」。這裡指他們假裝自己並非主動奪權。
generatedExample: He pretended to know the answer, but he was guessing.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意思是「奪取政權／掌握權力」，帶有突然或強硬取得的語感。
generatedExample: The rebels seized power after weeks of unrest.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，字面是「在轉角後面」，但這裡表示「很快就會到來」或「近在眼前」。
generatedExample: Summer vacation is just round the corner.

explanationZhTw: 動詞，意思是「放棄、讓出」。這裡指放棄權力。
generatedExample: She was reluctant to relinquish her seat on the committee.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的名詞意思是「手段、方法」，屬於較抽象的用法，不是「意思」。
generatedExample: Exercise is a means to better health.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 object 不是「物件」，而是「目的、目標」。
generatedExample: The object of the trip was to finish the survey.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `struck` 不是「打擊」，而是「對……有強烈印象／被……震到」的意思，屬於文學語境中的用法。
generatedExample: I was struck by how quiet the room felt.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `struck` 不是「打擊」，而是「對……有強烈印象／被……震到」的意思，屬於文學語境中的用法。
generatedExample: I was struck by how quiet the room felt.

explanationZhTw: `tiredness` 指「疲憊、倦容」，用來描述臉上顯出的疲態。
generatedExample: After the long trip, I could see the tiredness in her eyes.

explanationZhTw: `fleshy` 形容「肉感的、臉部或身體看起來有很多肉」，在這裡是外貌描寫。
generatedExample: He had a fleshy face and a deep voice.

explanationZhTw: `brutal` 在這裡不是單純「殘忍」，而是帶有「粗獷、強硬、帶壓迫感」的感覺。
generatedExample: The building's brutal design made it look imposing.

explanationZhTw: `helpless` 是「無能為力的、無法抗拒的」，表示人物面對對方的氣場時沒有辦法反抗。
generatedExample: I felt helpless when the lights went out in the storm.

explanationZhTw: `pouches` 在這裡指「眼下的浮腫袋、眼袋」，是身體外觀的具體描寫。
generatedExample: He had dark pouches under his eyes after the deadline.

explanationZhTw: `sagged` 指「下垂、鬆弛地往下掉」，常用來描述皮膚、布料或物體失去支撐。
generatedExample: The old sofa sagged in the middle.

explanationZhTw: `cheekbones` 是「顴骨」，屬於較具體的人體部位詞。
generatedExample: Her cheekbones became more visible as she lost weight.

explanationZhTw: `deliberately` 表示「故意地、刻意地」，這裡強調 O'Brien 的動作是有意為之。
generatedExample: She deliberately spoke more slowly so everyone could understand.

explanationZhTw: `worn` 在這裡不是「穿過的」，而是指「顯得疲憊、磨損似的」，常用來形容臉色或表情。
generatedExample: He looked worn after several nights without sleep.

explanationZhTw: 指「權力」或「支配力」，這裡是抽象的政治力量。
generatedExample: Power can change how people behave.

explanationZhTw: 指「阻止；防止」，使某事不要發生。
generatedExample: This medicine may prevent infection.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「腐爛」，而是指身體逐漸衰弱、敗壞。
generatedExample: Without care, the old building started to decay.

explanationZhTw: 指「個體；個人」，強調單一的人與整體相比。
generatedExample: Each individual has different needs.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，指「細胞」，也暗示個人只是整體中的一小部分。
generatedExample: A cell is the basic unit of life.

explanationZhTw: 指「疲憊；倦怠」，比一般的 tired 更正式。
generatedExample: After the long journey, she felt deep weariness.

explanationZhTw: 這裡仍是「細胞」的字面意思，但整句在比喻人體或整體生命系統。
generatedExample: The scientist examined the cell under a microscope.

explanationZhTw: 指「活力；生命力」，是較正式、文學性的說法。
generatedExample: She spoke with vigour and confidence.

explanationZhTw: 指「有機體；生物體」，這裡是較抽象的整體生命系統。
generatedExample: A tiny organism can survive in extreme heat.

explanationZhTw: `turn away` 這裡表示把身體或視線轉向另一邊，離開原本面對的方向。
generatedExample: She turned away from the window and sat down.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「閒逛」，而是帶有來回走動、踱步的意思，通常表示正在思考或不安。
generatedExample: He kept strolling up and down the hallway while waiting for the call.

explanationZhTw: 這是固定說法，意思是「就你而言／對你來說」。
generatedExample: So far as I am concerned, we can start tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: 「collective」在這裡是形容詞，意思是「集體的、由群體共同擁有的」。
generatedExample: The decision was collective, not made by one person alone.

explanationZhTw: 「occur to someone」意思是「突然想到、浮現在腦中」。
generatedExample: It occurred to me that we were looking at the wrong problem.

explanationZhTw: 「reversible」表示「可逆的、可翻轉成相反狀態的」。
generatedExample: This jacket is reversible, so you can wear either side out.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「defeated」不是只指比賽輸了，也指「被打敗、無法成功」。
generatedExample: Without support, the team felt defeated before the project began.

explanationZhTw: 「doomed」表示「註定要經歷某種不好的結果」。
generatedExample: The old bridge was doomed to collapse after years of damage.

explanationZhTw: 「utter」在這裡加強語氣，表示「完全的、徹底的」；整體是「完全屈服」。
generatedExample: The plan demanded utter submission to the new rules.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「merge himself in」不是單純「合併」，而是指「把自我融入、消融在某個群體中」。
generatedExample: He tried to merge himself in the new community and stop standing out.

explanationZhTw: 「immortal」意思是「不朽的、永生的」。
generatedExample: The legend says the hero became immortal after the final battle.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「matter」不是「事情」，而是「物質、物理世界」的意思。
generatedExample: Scientists study how matter behaves under extreme pressure.

explanationZhTw: 「absolute」表示「絕對的、完全的，沒有例外或限制」。
generatedExample: The manager had absolute authority over the final decision.

explanationZhTw: `ignore` 在這裡是「不理會、暫時不管」的意思，屬於常見但值得注意的動詞用法。
generatedExample: She ignored the message and kept working.

explanationZhTw: `violent` 在這裡不是指「暴力的行為」，而是修飾 `effort`，表示「強烈的、劇烈的」；整體是說他用了非常費力、近乎痛苦的力氣。
generatedExample: He made a violent effort to stand up after the fall.

explanationZhTw: `wrench` 在這裡是動詞，意思是「猛力扭動、拉扯到受傷」；不是一般名詞 `wrench`（扳手）的意思。
generatedExample: She wrenched her shoulder while lifting the box.

explanationZhTw: `control` 在這裡是「控制；支配；使按自己的意思運作」的基本用法。
generatedExample: I can control the brightness of the screen.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `matter` 指「物質；材料」，不是一般常見的「事情、問題」。
generatedExample: All things around us are made of matter.

explanationZhTw: `burst out` 指「突然脫口而出；激動地大聲說」。這裡表示他情緒突然湧上來，忍不住說出口。
generatedExample: She burst out laughing when she heard the joke.

explanationZhTw: `control` 在這裡仍是「控制、左右」的意思；用來說明人無法支配自然現象。
generatedExample: No one can control the weather.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「在頭骨裡」；這裡比喻「在腦中、在思想裡」。
generatedExample: For him, the whole world exists inside the skull.

explanationZhTw: 指「逐漸地、一步一步地」，不是一次完成。
generatedExample: She improved by degrees as she practiced every day.

explanationZhTw: 指「懸浮、漂浮」；這裡是超自然地讓身體升空。
generatedExample: The magician claimed he could perform levitation.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「飄離、升離」；不是一般的「漂浮」而已。
generatedExample: The balloon seemed to float off into the evening sky.

explanationZhTw: 表示「擺脫、丟掉、去除」，可用在物品、想法或習慣上。
generatedExample: I need to get rid of these old files.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「主人」；在這個語境中是指「掌控者、統治者」。整句是在說對方還沒有真正控制這個星球。
generatedExample: They act like masters of the project, but the client still makes the final decisions.

explanationZhTw: 「行星；星球」。這裡指地球或整個世界。
generatedExample: Scientists found a new planet far beyond our solar system.

explanationZhTw: 「征服；擊敗並控制」。這裡通常帶有軍事、政治或權力上的取得控制權的意思。
generatedExample: The army conquered the city after a long siege.

explanationZhTw: 「conquer」在這裡是「征服、擊敗並控制」的意思，屬於較正式、帶有強烈支配意味的用法。
generatedExample: They hoped to conquer the region before winter.

explanationZhTw: 「suits us」在這裡不是指衣服合身，而是指「對我們方便、符合我們的時機或利益」。
generatedExample: We can leave now if it suits us.

explanationZhTw: 這是反問語氣，意思是「那又有什麼差別呢？」用來表示認為結果無關緊要。
generatedExample: If they arrive late, what difference would it make?

explanationZhTw: 這裡是非常強烈的說法，意思是「把他們徹底消滅，讓他們不復存在」。
generatedExample: The regime tried to shut every trace of the movement out of existence.

explanationZhTw: 「speck of dust」字面是「一粒灰塵」，這裡用來比喻世界非常渺小，強調不重要或微不足道的感覺。
generatedExample: In the vast universe, our planet is just a speck of dust.

explanationZhTw: 這個連字複合詞不是固定片語，但在這裡表示「又渺小又無助」，是帶有強烈語氣的描述。
generatedExample: After the storm, the travelers felt tiny-helpless in the dark.

explanationZhTw: 「in existence」表示「存在、活著、出現於世上」，這裡是在問人類已經存在多久。
generatedExample: This museum has been in existence for more than a century.

explanationZhTw: 「uninhabited」表示「無人居住的、沒有人定居的」，常用來描述地方或地球在某段時間內沒有生命或居民。
generatedExample: The island looked beautiful but was completely uninhabited.

explanationZhTw: 用來表示「胡說」「荒謬」，這裡是直接否定對方說法的語氣。
generatedExample: That's nonsense; the plan will not work as written.

explanationZhTw: 指「地球」；這裡是基本字面用法。
generatedExample: The earth orbits the sun once a year.

explanationZhTw: 指「人的意識」或「人的覺知」；這裡帶有哲學意味，表示事物的存在依賴於人的意識。
generatedExample: He argued that reality is shaped by human consciousness.

explanationZhTw: 「extinct」指「已滅絕的」，用來形容某個物種已經完全不存在於世上。這裡是在描述化石骨骼所屬的古代動物。
generatedExample: Dinosaurs are extinct, but their bones still fascinate scientists.

explanationZhTw: 「be heard of」在這裡是慣用說法，意思是「被人知道、被人聽說過」。整句是在強調那是人類尚未出現、也還沒有人類被認知的非常久遠年代。
generatedExample: That company is so new that no one has ever heard of it yet.

explanationZhTw: biologists 指「生物學家」，也就是研究生命與生物的科學家。
generatedExample: Biologists compared the DNA of the two species.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 invented 不是「發明器物」，而是「捏造、編出來」，意思是這些骨頭是人為虛構的。
generatedExample: The story was invented to make the rumor sound believable.

explanationZhTw: come to an end 是固定說法，表示「結束、終止、走向消亡」。
generatedExample: The concert came to an end just before midnight.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 Outside 不是單純指「外面」，而是指「超出人類之外、與人類無關的範圍」。
generatedExample: Outside our team, nobody knew the plan.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「outside us」不只是字面上的「在我們外面」，也帶有「超出我們自身、超出我們能掌握的範圍」的意思。
generatedExample: Some problems are outside us and cannot be solved by wishful thinking alone.

explanationZhTw: 「light-years」原本是天文距離單位「光年」；這裡用來強調距離極其遙遠。
generatedExample: The nearest galaxy is millions of light-years away.

explanationZhTw: 「out of our reach」表示「我們無法碰到、取得或影響到」，這裡強調那些星星永遠遙不可及。
generatedExample: That house is out of our reach, so we need to look for something cheaper.

explanationZhTw: 「indifferently」在這裡是「冷淡地、漫不經心地」的意思，表示說話時沒有興趣或情緒起伏。
generatedExample: He answered indifferently, as if the question did not matter.

explanationZhTw: 「blot out」是片語動詞，意思是「遮住、使看不見、抹去」。
generatedExample: The clouds blotted out the moon.

explanationZhTw: 「go round」是片語動詞，這裡表示「繞著……運行／環繞」。
generatedExample: The planets go round the sun.

explanationZhTw: 「convulsive」指「抽搐的、痙攣似的」，這裡不是一般的「快速」或「突然」，而是帶有身體不受控制的動作感，強調 Winston 的緊繃與失常狀態。
generatedExample: She gave a convulsive laugh and then went silent.

explanationZhTw: 「navigate」在這裡指「航行、導航」；也就是依靠方向或航線在海上前進。
generatedExample: The captain knew how to navigate the narrow channel at night.

explanationZhTw: 「predict」是「預測」；這裡指提前判斷日蝕或月蝕會發生。
generatedExample: Scientists can predict an eclipse years in advance.

explanationZhTw: 「assume」表示「假定、先當作是真的」；不一定已經證實。
generatedExample: We should not assume that everyone has the same background.

explanationZhTw: 「beyond us」表示「超出我們的能力範圍」；也就是我們做不到。
generatedExample: Fixing the engine alone was beyond us.

explanationZhTw: 「dual system」是「雙重體系、雙套系統」；這裡指同時保留兩套不同的天文說法。
generatedExample: The company operated a dual system during the transition.

explanationZhTw: 「according as」是較正式、較少見的用法，意思是「視……而定、依照……」；結果取決於情況。
generatedExample: We will adjust the budget according as sales improve.

explanationZhTw: 「unequal to that」表示「沒有能力做到那件事」；這裡是較文學或古典的說法。
generatedExample: I am unequal to such a difficult task without help.

explanationZhTw: 「doublethink」是奧威爾創造的詞，指同時接受兩個互相矛盾的信念，卻不覺得衝突。
generatedExample: The novel uses doublethink to show how propaganda can distort reality.

explanationZhTw: 指「退縮、往後縮」，這裡不是單純的身體後退，而是帶有害怕、受壓迫而退避的感覺。
generatedExample: She shrank back when the dog barked at her.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，表示那個回答像鈍器重擊一樣把他打擊得很難受、很受挫，不是字面上的「壓碎」。
generatedExample: His blunt rejection crushed me like a bludgeon.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示「自己是對的、站在正確的一方」。
generatedExample: She was sure she was in the right during the argument.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指「謬誤、錯誤的論證或觀念」。在這裡是哲學式或邏輯上的「錯誤想法」。
generatedExample: The idea turned out to be a fallacy after further study.

explanationZhTw: 「形而上學」；這裡指抽象哲學概念。
generatedExample: She studied metaphysics to understand questions about reality and existence.

explanationZhTw: 「強項、擅長的地方」；這裡是否定說話者擅長形而上學。
generatedExample: Math is not my strong point, so I ask for help with equations.

explanationZhTw: 「唯我論」；哲學上認為只有自己的心靈或意識最確定存在。
generatedExample: His essay discussed solipsism and the problem of knowing other minds.

explanationZhTw: 「集體唯我論」；在這裡是作者自造的說法，指一個群體把自己的想法當成唯一真實。
generatedExample: The leader's collective solipsism made the whole team ignore outside evidence.

explanationZhTw: 「離題、插話」；指談話暫時偏離主題。
generatedExample: Her story turned into a digression about her childhood.

explanationZhTw: 「為某事奮鬥、爭取」；這裡表示必須持續努力去得到。
generatedExample: They had to fight for better working conditions.

explanationZhTw: 「帶有……的神情／姿態」；這裡指他擺出像老師一樣的態度。
generatedExample: She spoke with an air of confidence, even though she was nervous.

explanationZhTw: 「宣示、施加自己的權力於別人身上」；這裡的 assert 不是單純「斷言」，而是「展現並行使權力」。
generatedExample: The boss tried to assert his power over the new employees.

explanationZhTw: `thought` 是 `think` 的過去式，表示「想到、思考、認為」。這裡是很常見的基本用法。
generatedExample: I thought about your question for a minute before answering.

explanationZhTw: `suffer` 表示「受苦、遭受痛苦」。在這裡是直接的字面意思，指讓某人承受痛苦或折磨。
generatedExample: She suffered a lot after the accident.

explanationZhTw: `inflict` 表示「造成、加諸（痛苦、傷害等）」；這裡指以施加痛苦和羞辱來展現權力。
generatedExample: The new policy inflicts extra costs on small businesses.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，表示把人的心智徹底摧毀、拆解，再按自己的意思重塑。
generatedExample: The constant pressure tore her confidence to pieces.

explanationZhTw: `abolish` 表示「廢除、取消」；這裡指強行取消一種人類本能與快感。
generatedExample: The city plans to abolish the old parking rule next year.

explanationZhTw: `intoxication` 原本是「中毒、醉態」，這裡是比喻，指權力帶來的強烈亢奮與陶醉感。
generatedExample: He could not hide the intoxication of victory after the final whistle.

explanationZhTw: `trample on` 在這裡是片語動詞，表示「踐踏、肆意凌辱」；不是單純踩在上面，而是帶有支配和羞辱意味。
generatedExample: They were accused of trampling on workers' rights.

explanationZhTw: 表示「停頓、暫停動作或說話」。這裡是很一般的動詞，用來描寫 O'Brien 突然不再說話。
generatedExample: She paused before answering the question.

explanationZhTw: 表示「往後縮、退縮」，通常因為害怕、尷尬或想避開某事。這裡是身體和心理上的退縮感。
generatedExample: When the dog barked, he shrank back a step.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「結冰」，而是比喻心裡像被凍住一樣，表示極度恐懼、麻木或無法反應。
generatedExample: When she heard the news, she felt frozen for a moment.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上「被蓋章」，而是指被踐踏、被凌辱。
generatedExample: He spoke as if his dignity was something to be stamped upon.

explanationZhTw: 指「異端者」，也就是違背正統、被視為危險思想的人。
generatedExample: The old writer was treated like a heretic for challenging the official story.

explanationZhTw: 指名聲或可信度被削弱、被抹黑。
generatedExample: The report was discredited after the sources turned out to be false.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 play out 不是「玩完」，而是指一段過程被完整演完、一路走完。
generatedExample: The argument played out in public over several weeks.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「任憑我們處置」或「完全受制於我們」。
generatedExample: With no phone signal and no map, we were at the mountain guide's mercy.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是指身心被摧垮、變得支離破碎，不是單純「分開」。
generatedExample: After months of pressure, he looked completely broken up.

explanationZhTw: 表示「自己主動、出於自願」，不是被逼迫。
generatedExample: She returned the money of her own accord.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「恢復鎮定、冷靜下來」，不是字面上的「把自己找回來」。
generatedExample: After the shock, she recovered herself and continued the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是副詞，表示「虛弱地、無力地」，用來描述說話的方式。
generatedExample: He laughed weakly and leaned against the wall.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `mean by` 不是單純的「意思是」，而是「你指的是什麼／你想表達什麼」；整句是在追問對方那句話真正的意思。
generatedExample: What do you mean by that symbol on the map?

explanationZhTw: 「create」在這裡是「創造、製造」的意思，表示把某種東西從無到有地做出來；在這句中是較常見、基礎的動詞用法。
generatedExample: She wants to create a world where everyone feels safe.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「dream」不是「睡夢」的字面意思，而是指「想像中的事物、虛構不實的想法」；屬於依上下文而變化的意思。
generatedExample: His plan sounds nice, but it may just be a dream.

explanationZhTw: 「impossible」表示「不可能的」，用來強調某事無法實現或做不到；是非常常見的基礎形容詞。
generatedExample: Without water, it is impossible to live there.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `found` 不是「找到」，而是「建立、創立」。在這句話中，意思是「不可能在恐懼、仇恨和殘酷之上建立文明」。
generatedExample: They hoped to found a new school in the city center.

explanationZhTw: `endure` 在這裡是「持續存在、長久維持」的意思，不是單純「忍受」。句子表示這種文明不可能長久存在。
generatedExample: Without support, the project would not endure for long.

explanationZhTw: 口語常用的反問式回應，意思是「為什麼不呢？」或「好啊／可以啊」。通常用來表示同意、接受提議，或覺得某件事沒有阻礙。
generatedExample: "Do you want to try the new cafe?" "Why not?"

explanationZhTw: 「活力、生命力」。這裡指某事物失去生氣、活力或維持下去的能量。
generatedExample: The plan had no vitality after the first few setbacks.

explanationZhTw: 「瓦解、崩解」。這裡是比喻用法，不是指真的碎成小片，而是指整體開始分崩離析。
generatedExample: If the pressure keeps building, the alliance will disintegrate.

explanationZhTw: 「自殺」。這是固定搭配，表示以自己的行為結束生命。
generatedExample: The story ends when he says he might commit suicide.

explanationZhTw: 「under the impression」表示「以為、誤以為」；這裡是在指出對方有一個不一定正確的看法。
generatedExample: I was under the impression that the train left at 8:00.

explanationZhTw: 「wear ourselves out」表示「把自己弄得筋疲力盡、耗盡體力或精力」。
generatedExample: If we keep working without rest, we will wear ourselves out.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「quicken the tempo」是「加快節奏、加快步調」的意思，用來比喻讓整個生活速度變快。
generatedExample: The company wants to quicken the tempo of product development.

explanationZhTw: 「senile」原指「因老化而心智衰退的、老態龍鍾的」；這裡是誇張地說人三十歲就像進入老年衰退狀態。
generatedExample: The speaker made a cruel joke about becoming senile before old age.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「party」不是「派對」，而是指「政黨」；在小說語境中，特別是執政組織。
generatedExample: The party claimed that it alone could protect the nation.

explanationZhTw: 「immortal」表示「不朽的、永存的」；這裡強調這個政黨似乎永遠不會被消滅。
generatedExample: Her songs have made her name feel immortal.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `battered` 不是單純「敲打」，而是比喻成聲音的壓迫把 Winston 完全擊潰，讓他失去反抗能力。
generatedExample: The constant criticism battered her into silence.

explanationZhTw: `in dread` 表示「非常恐懼、提心吊膽」，比一般的 `afraid` 更強烈，也帶有持續不安的感覺。
generatedExample: He was in dread of hearing the final result.

explanationZhTw: `persisted in` 表示「堅持繼續做某事」，這裡是指 Winston 仍然持續反對對方的說法。
generatedExample: She persisted in asking questions until she got an answer.

explanationZhTw: `feebly` 表示「虛弱地、無力地」，在這裡也暗示他發言時缺乏信心與力量。
generatedExample: He smiled feebly and sat back down.

explanationZhTw: `inarticulate` 是「無法清楚表達的、說不出口的」；`inarticulate horror` 指一種難以用語言說明的強烈恐懼或厭惡。
generatedExample: She could only stare at him in inarticulate horror.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「回到攻擊」，而是比喻成「重新展開反駁／再度進攻」。
generatedExample: After a short pause, he returned to the attack in the debate.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「我不在乎／我無所謂」，是很常見的固定說法。
generatedExample: I don't care which restaurant we choose.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「不知怎地／總之」，帶出一種無法避免的結果。
generatedExample: Somehow I found my way home in the rain.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「失敗；未達到預期」的意思。
generatedExample: If you never practice, you may fail the test.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純比賽輸贏，而是「打敗、擊垮」；語氣很強，帶有被人生壓垮的感覺。
generatedExample: A lack of sleep will defeat me today.

explanationZhTw: 指「在所有層面／所有階段」，不是只控制部分。
generatedExample: This policy affects the company at all its levels.

explanationZhTw: 表示「被激怒、感到極度憤怒或受冒犯」。
generatedExample: Many readers were outraged by the decision.

explanationZhTw: 表示「反過來敵對我們、倒向對立面」。
generatedExample: If we keep ignoring their concerns, they may turn against us.

explanationZhTw: 指「人性／人類本性」；這裡是在說連人性都可以被塑造。
generatedExample: The novel asks whether human nature can really change.

explanationZhTw: 表示「可塑的、容易被改變的」。
generatedExample: Young people are often more malleable than adults.

explanationZhTw: 指「無產階級的人」；這是馬克思主義語境的詞。
generatedExample: The pamphlet was written for the proletarians of the city.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「起身」，而是「起來反抗、起事」。
generatedExample: People may arise when they feel they have no other choice.

explanationZhTw: 表示「推翻我們、顛覆我們的統治」。
generatedExample: The rebels tried to overthrow the government.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「別再想了／把這件事忘掉」。
generatedExample: It is over now, so try to put it out of your mind.

explanationZhTw: 表示「無助的、無法反抗的」。
generatedExample: Without support, the refugees felt helpless.

explanationZhTw: 這是刻意造出的詞，意思接近「在體制外毫無意義、不值得考慮」。
generatedExample: To him, criticism from outside-irrelevant voices did not matter.

explanationZhTw: 「in the end」表示「最後、終究」，常用來說明事情的最終結果。
generatedExample: In the end, we chose the simpler option.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「beat you」不是「打你一頓」，而是「打敗你、擊垮你」。
generatedExample: Our team beat you by one point.

explanationZhTw: 「sooner or later」表示「遲早」，意思是某件事最後一定會發生。
generatedExample: Sooner or later, you will need to make a decision.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「看穿你真正的樣子／本質」，不是單純的「看見你」。
generatedExample: People will eventually see me for what I am.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，字面是「把你撕成碎片」，實際上常指「把你徹底擊垮、狠狠批評或摧毀」。
generatedExample: The critics will tear the new movie to pieces.

explanationZhTw: 「evidence」在這裡是指「證據、跡象」，用來支持某件事真的正在發生。
generatedExample: There is no evidence that the plan is working.

explanationZhTw: 「any reason why...」在這裡是「有任何理由……嗎」，用來詢問某件事是否有理由成立或發生。
generatedExample: Can you think of any reason why she left early?

explanationZhTw: 相信；在這裡表示主觀上認定某事為真。
generatedExample: I believe you can finish it on time.

explanationZhTw: 失敗；在這裡指無法達成目標或無法做到。
generatedExample: If I don't study, I will fail the test.

explanationZhTw: 宇宙；這裡不是日常口語中的單一物體，而是指整個存在世界，語氣帶有哲學意味。
generatedExample: Scientists still have many questions about the universe.

explanationZhTw: 靈體；在這裡不是「精神」的常見抽象義，而是指某種超自然存在或力量。
generatedExample: People said there was a spirit in the old house.

explanationZhTw: 原則；這裡不是一般規則條文，而是指某種支配萬物的抽象法則或根本原理。
generatedExample: She tries to live by a clear principle of honesty.

explanationZhTw: 克服；在這裡表示戰勝或超越某種阻力、力量或限制。
generatedExample: With practice, you can overcome your fear of speaking.

explanationZhTw: 「believe in」在這裡不是單純「相信」某件事，而是「信仰」某種宗教或神祇。
generatedExample: Many people believe in a higher power.

explanationZhTw: 常見的否定回答，表示拒絕、不同意或回答「不是」。
generatedExample: He asked for help, but she simply said, 'No.'

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `principle` 不是一般抽象的「原則」而已，而是指一套會主導行動、造成結果的核心觀念或規則。
generatedExample: The company changed its guiding principle to focus on customer trust.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `defeat` 不是單純「打敗」某人，而是指某種想法、制度或力量讓我們失敗、失去優勢。
generatedExample: If we ignore the warning signs, the problem will defeat us eventually.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是「精神狀態」的日常意思，而是指「人類的本質／靈魂」，屬於比較抽象、帶哲學意味的用法。
generatedExample: She believed the spirit of the city was still alive after the storm.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Man` 大寫，不是指單一男性，而是泛指「人類」；這是文學或哲學語境中常見的用法。
generatedExample: They spoke about the future of Man and the choices it must make.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `consider yourself` 是「認為自己是／把自己視為」的意思，不是「考慮你自己」。
generatedExample: Do you consider yourself ready for the interview?

explanationZhTw: 指「最後一個人／最後的倖存者」。這裡是比喻用法，強調溫斯頓被完全孤立，像是這一類人中最後留下的一個。
generatedExample: After the disaster, he felt like the last man in the city.

explanationZhTw: 指「滅絕的、絕跡的」。原本多用來說物種不存在了；這裡把它用在人類／一群人身上，帶有強烈的比喻意味。
generatedExample: Many species became extinct long before humans recorded them.

explanationZhTw: 指「繼承者；承接前人遺產或地位的人」。這裡不是只說財產繼承，而是指接手整個世界／未來的人。
generatedExample: We are the inheritors of everything our parents built.

explanationZhTw: 指「不在歷史之中、彷彿不被歷史記錄或承認」。這裡是很特殊的語境用法，表示他被排除在人類歷史進程之外。
generatedExample: Without any records, their struggle seemed outside history.

explanationZhTw: 指「不存在的；被當成不存在」。這裡不是單純描述事實，而是帶有抹除、否認其存在的意味。
generatedExample: In that report, the problem was treated as non-existent.

explanationZhTw: 指「自認在道德上比別人高一等」。這裡帶有諷刺和指控的語氣，暗示對方自以為正義。
generatedExample: She spoke as if she were morally superior to everyone else.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「認為；把……視為」。句中是說「我認為自己比別人優越」。
generatedExample: I consider her a trusted friend.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是形容詞，表示「優越的；比別人更好的」。在這句話中帶有自認高人一等的意思。
generatedExample: He thinks his method is superior to the others.

explanationZhTw: 指「聲軌、錄音內容」；這裡是把對話錄下來的聲音記錄。
generatedExample: The sound-track of the interview was clear enough to hear every word.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思是「加入」某個組織，不是一般學校的註冊入學。
generatedExample: She enrolled herself in the volunteer group after reading the notice.

explanationZhTw: 指「偽造」文件、簽名或金錢。
generatedExample: Someone forged his signature on the contract.

explanationZhTw: 指「散播、傳播」資訊或疾病。
generatedExample: The app helps disseminate safety alerts quickly.

explanationZhTw: 指「性病」；是較正式、較少在日常口語中使用的說法。
generatedExample: The clinic offers testing and treatment for venereal diseases.

explanationZhTw: 字面上是「硫酸」或腐蝕性液體；這裡指拿酸去潑人臉的暴力行為。
generatedExample: The attacker threatened to throw vitriol at the victim.

explanationZhTw: 「get up」是片語動詞，意思是起床、起身；這裡是命令語氣，要對方從床上起來。
generatedExample: I usually get up at 7 a.m. on weekdays.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `bonds` 不是金融上的「債券」，而是指束縛、鎖鏈或限制。
generatedExample: The bonds around his wrists were finally cut.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示束縛變鬆了；`themselves` 帶有擬人化效果，讓畫面更強烈。
generatedExample: The rope loosened itself after the knot slipped.

explanationZhTw: `stand up` 是常見片語動詞，意思是從坐著、躺著或蹲著的狀態起身站立。
generatedExample: She stood up when the teacher entered the room.

explanationZhTw: 這個副詞表示「不穩地、搖晃地」，描述動作缺乏平衡或力量。
generatedExample: After the long run, he walked unsteadily to the bench.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「the last man」不是字面上「最後一個男人」，而是指最後仍保有人性、能抵抗的人；屬於依上下文才看得懂的說法。
generatedExample: In the story, she felt like the last man standing against the regime.

explanationZhTw: 「guardian」是「守護者、保護者」；整個片語在文中是把某人描寫成捍衛人性核心的人，帶有比字面更抽象的意思。
generatedExample: The teacher became a guardian of the school's culture.

explanationZhTw: 「take off」是片語動詞，這裡表示「脫掉」衣物，不是「起飛」或「離開」。
generatedExample: Please take off your shoes before entering the room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `undid` 不是「撤銷」的抽象用法，而是指把繩子解開、鬆開，屬於日常動作的具體意思。
generatedExample: She undid the knot and opened the package.

explanationZhTw: `zip fastener` 指拉鍊，是衣物上的常見配件。
generatedExample: My jacket's zip fastener is stuck.

explanationZhTw: `wrenched out` 表示用力硬扯出來，帶有粗暴、強力移除的意思。
generatedExample: The old nail was wrenched out of the wood.

explanationZhTw: `stopped short` 是慣用語，表示走到一半突然停住，通常因為驚訝、害怕或發現異常。
generatedExample: When she saw the broken glass, she stopped short.

explanationZhTw: `broken out` 這裡不是常見的「爆發」事件用法，而是指聲音突然不由自主地冒出來。
generatedExample: A laugh broke out before he could answer.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞；在這裡表示「繼續說／繼續做」，不是字面上的「往前走」。
generatedExample: Go on, tell me what happened next.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 wings 不是「翅膀」，而是指物體兩側像翅膀般伸出的「側片／側翼」。
generatedExample: The mirror had two folding wings on each side.

explanationZhTw: 害怕的；受驚的。
generatedExample: She looked frightened when the lights went out.

explanationZhTw: 像骷髏般的；非常瘦削的。
generatedExample: After weeks of illness, he looked skeleton-like.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單指「玻璃」材料，而是指可映照影像的玻璃面或鏡面。
generatedExample: She checked her hair in the glass by the door.

explanationZhTw: 向前突出；伸出。這裡形容臉部因姿勢而顯得往前凸。
generatedExample: The bolt protruded from the wall.

explanationZhTw: 顯示出來；反映出來。這裡不是「登記」，而是指臉上表現出情緒。
generatedExample: Her face registered shock before she could speak.

explanationZhTw: 部分禿頭的；頭髮掉了一部分。
generatedExample: He was partially bald by the age of forty.

explanationZhTw: 深深嵌入、積久難除的污垢。
generatedExample: The boots were covered in ingrained dirt.

explanationZhTw: 靜脈曲張性潰瘍；常見於腿部的慢性傷口。
generatedExample: The nurse cleaned the varicose ulcer carefully.

explanationZhTw: 一片片脫落；剝落。
generatedExample: The old paint was peeling off the wall.

explanationZhTw: 極度消瘦；骨瘦如柴。
generatedExample: The doctor was alarmed by the patient's emaciation.

explanationZhTw: 側面；從側面看的樣子。
generatedExample: The side view of the building is more impressive than the front.

explanationZhTw: 彎曲度；曲率。
generatedExample: The curvature of the road makes overtaking difficult.

explanationZhTw: 前傾、駝著背。
generatedExample: He hunched forward to read the small print.

explanationZhTw: 憑猜測；大概是。
generatedExample: At a guess, the train is already ten minutes late.

explanationZhTw: 惡性疾病；通常指癌症等嚴重疾病。
generatedExample: The scans showed no sign of malignant disease.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「認為、想過」的意思，不是名詞「想法」。
generatedExample: I thought about your suggestion last night.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 face 指「外表、容貌」，不是字面上單獨的「臉」部位用法。
generatedExample: Her face looked tired after the long trip.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 face 指「外表、容貌」，不是字面上單獨的「臉」部位用法。
generatedExample: Her face looked tired after the long trip.

explanationZhTw: 專有名詞，指《一九八四》裡的「內黨」高層統治集團。
generatedExample: The Inner Party controls the most important decisions.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「顯得疲憊、老態、磨損」的意思，不只是「穿破的」。
generatedExample: After months of stress, he looked worn and pale.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 face 指「外表、容貌」，不是字面上單獨的「臉」部位用法。
generatedExample: Her face looked tired after the long trip.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `seized` 不是「抓住」到法律或奪取的抽象用法，而是指突然用力抓住某人，帶有強硬、迅速的動作感。
generatedExample: She seized his arm before he could walk away.

explanationZhTw: `spun him round` 指把某人快速轉過身來；在這裡是直接的動作片語，不是字面上真的旋轉他，而是把身體方向猛然轉向另一邊。
generatedExample: The guard spun him round and pointed to the door.

explanationZhTw: condition 指「狀況、情形」；這裡是指某人的身體狀態。
generatedExample: The doctor asked about my condition after the surgery.

explanationZhTw: filthy 是「非常髒的」，grime 是「積很久、很難清掉的污垢」；合起來強調極度骯髒。
generatedExample: There was filthy grime on the windowsill.

explanationZhTw: running sore 指會滲液、反覆發作的傷口或潰瘍，不是一般小擦傷。
generatedExample: The nurse cleaned the running sore carefully.

explanationZhTw: 這是誇張比喻，意思是「臭得很厲害」。
generatedExample: His shoes stink like a goat after soccer practice.

explanationZhTw: cease to do something 表示「停止做某事」；這裡是「已經不再注意」。
generatedExample: After a few minutes, I ceased to notice the noise.

explanationZhTw: emaciation 指因疾病、飢餓或長期消耗而「極度消瘦」的狀態。
generatedExample: The photograph showed her emaciation after months of illness.

explanationZhTw: in our hands 這裡不是字面意思，而是「在我們控制之下、由我們掌握」。
generatedExample: With the contract signed, the decision is in our hands.

explanationZhTw: come out 這裡指頭髮「掉落、脫落」，不是一般的「出來」。
generatedExample: My hair is coming out more than usual this winter.

explanationZhTw: pluck at 表示「輕扯、揪了一下」；這裡指用手把頭髮拉下來。
generatedExample: She plucked at the loose thread on her sleeve.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「seized」不是一般的「抓到」，而是帶有用力、突然、甚至粗暴地「猛抓住」的意思。
generatedExample: She seized my arm before I could fall.

explanationZhTw: 「remaining」表示「剩下的、尚存的」，指在前面那些牙齒之後，還留著的牙。
generatedExample: We ate the remaining apples after dinner.

explanationZhTw: 「forefinger」是「食指」；這是較具體的解剖用語。
generatedExample: He pointed at the map with his forefinger.

explanationZhTw: 「twinge of pain」指「一陣刺痛／抽痛」，通常是短暫、突然出現的痛感。
generatedExample: I felt a twinge of pain when I twisted my ankle.

explanationZhTw: 「shot through」在這裡不是字面上的「射穿」，而是形容疼痛快速地「竄過」某個部位。
generatedExample: A chill shot through her body when she heard the noise.

explanationZhTw: 「wrenched」表示用力猛拉、硬扯，帶有強迫和疼痛的感覺。
generatedExample: He wrenched the door open in a panic.

explanationZhTw: 「tossed」是「隨手丟、輕拋」；比起正式的 throw，更帶有不在意的感覺。
generatedExample: She tossed the keys onto the table.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指肉體腐爛，而是比喻整個人正在迅速敗壞、崩潰。
generatedExample: After months without sleep, he felt like he was rotting away.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，指精神、情緒或狀態逐漸瓦解、崩潰。
generatedExample: When the deadline hit, the whole plan fell to pieces.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是轉身、轉過去。
generatedExample: Turn around so I can check the back of your shirt.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「最後剩下的人／最後的倖存者」，不是字面上單指男性。
generatedExample: After the disaster, he thought he was the last man left.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 humanity 指「人類」或「人性」中的前者，也就是整個人類。
generatedExample: The statue seemed to represent the future of humanity.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是穿上衣服。
generatedExample: It is cold outside, so put your coat on before you leave.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `stirred` 不是「攪動」，而是「浮現、激起」的意思，表示念頭突然出現並強烈牽動他。
generatedExample: A strange question stirred in my mind during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `fixed` 不是「修理」，而是「整理、把...弄好」破爛衣物的意思。
generatedExample: She fixed her scarf around her neck before stepping outside.

explanationZhTw: `overcame` 在這裡是「壓倒、淹沒」他的意思，表示某種情緒強烈到他無法抵抗。
generatedExample: A wave of exhaustion overcame me after the long walk.

explanationZhTw: `collapsed on to` 是片語動詞，表示「倒下、癱坐到」某處，這裡帶有突然失去支撐的感覺。
generatedExample: He collapsed onto the sofa after running up the stairs.

explanationZhTw: `burst into tears` 是慣用語，表示「突然大哭起來」。
generatedExample: When she heard the news, she burst into tears.

explanationZhTw: `aware of` 表示「意識到、察覺到」，這裡是他清楚感受到自己的醜陋與狼狽。
generatedExample: I was aware of the noise outside, but I kept working.

explanationZhTw: `gracelessness` 指「笨拙、不雅、缺乏優雅」，語氣偏文學，形容動作或姿態不好看。
generatedExample: The dancer's gracelessness made her feel self-conscious.

explanationZhTw: 「last for ever」表示「永遠持續下去、一直不結束」。這裡是在說某件事不會無限延續。
generatedExample: This pain will not last forever.

explanationZhTw: 「escape from」是片語動詞，意思是「從……逃離、擺脫」。在這裡指你可以離開或擺脫那件事。
generatedExample: She escaped from the crowded room.

explanationZhTw: 「depends on」表示「取決於；由……決定」。這裡強調結果完全看你自己。
generatedExample: The result depends on your decision.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「做到了」，而是帶有責怪語氣的「都是你害的／是你造成的」。
generatedExample: You did it, and now we have to fix the mess together.

explanationZhTw: 「sobbed」是指「抽泣著說／哽咽著說」，表示說話時帶著哭聲。
generatedExample: She sobbed while telling her friend the news.

explanationZhTw: 「reduce someone to something」在這裡是慣用說法，意思是把某人逼成、弄成現在這種糟糕狀態。
generatedExample: The pressure reduced him to tears before the meeting ended.

explanationZhTw: 「把自己降格到那種地步／淪落到那一步」；這裡表示是你自己把自己推向那種處境，帶有強烈責備意味。
generatedExample: You reduced yourself to it by choosing to lie again and again.

explanationZhTw: 「與……對抗、挑戰……」；這是片語動詞式用法，表示主動把自己放到對立面。
generatedExample: If you set yourself up against the whole team, you will be isolated.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「contained」不是單純的「包含」，而是指「已經內含、預先決定地被包在裡面」；屬於較抽象的語境用法。
generatedExample: The answer was contained in the first clue all along.

explanationZhTw: 「預見、預料到」；比一般常見的「see」更正式，表示事先判斷某事會發生。
generatedExample: She could foresee the problems before the project even started.

explanationZhTw: 「went on」在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「繼續說／繼續進行」。句子中表示他先停了一下，然後接著說下去。
generatedExample: She hesitated for a moment, and then went on with her story.

explanationZhTw: `beat` 在這裡指「打」；`beaten` 表示「被打過、被毆打」。
generatedExample: He had been beaten before the police arrived.

explanationZhTw: `break up` 在這裡不是「分手」，而是指把一個人徹底打垮、摧毀到精神或身體都崩潰。
generatedExample: The constant pressure really broke him up.

explanationZhTw: `kick` 在這裡是「踢」，`kicked` 表示「被踢」。
generatedExample: He was kicked during the fight.

explanationZhTw: `flogged` 指「用鞭子抽打」，比一般的 `hit` 更強烈、也更正式。
generatedExample: In the story, the prisoner was flogged for speaking out.

explanationZhTw: `whimper` 是「嗚咽、哀鳴」；`for mercy` 表示「求饒、請對方手下留情」。
generatedExample: The child whimpered for mercy after falling over.

explanationZhTw: `betrayed` 是「背叛」，這裡指對他人或理念不忠、出賣他們。
generatedExample: She felt betrayed when her friend shared the secret.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `degradation` 不是單純「品質下降」，而是指「屈辱、人格上的貶抑」。
generatedExample: He described the humiliations and degradation of prison life.

explanationZhTw: 「weeping」是「哭泣、流淚」；比一般的「crying」更正式、文學感更強。
generatedExample: After the phone call, she sat there weeping for several minutes.

explanationZhTw: 「oozing」原意是「滲出、慢慢流出」；這裡形容眼淚還在慢慢從眼睛裡流出，帶有強烈畫面感。
generatedExample: Blood was oozing from a small cut on his arm.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡，`betrayed` 不是單純的「背叛感情」而已，而是指「違背對 Julia 的忠誠、把她出賣或對不起她」。這是帶有強烈道德與關係色彩的用法，常見於小說或嚴肅敘事中。
generatedExample: He felt he had betrayed his friends by telling the secret.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `betrayed` 不是「背叛感情」的狹義用法，而是指「出賣、洩露、對某人不忠」；在這句話中，意思是「你沒有把茱莉亞供出來／沒有出賣茱莉亞」。
generatedExample: She betrayed her friend's confidence by telling others the secret.

explanationZhTw: 「reverence」指崇敬、敬仰，帶有強烈的尊重與仰慕。
generatedExample: She spoke of her mentor with reverence.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「flooded」不是字面上的淹水，而是形容情緒突然充滿、湧上心頭。
generatedExample: A sense of relief flooded him after the call.

explanationZhTw: 「intelligent」表示聰明的、理解力強的。
generatedExample: Her questions were sharp and intelligent.

explanationZhTw: 「intelligent」表示聰明的、理解力強的。
generatedExample: Her questions were sharp and intelligent.

explanationZhTw: 「promptly」表示迅速地、立刻地。
generatedExample: He replied promptly to the email.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「betrayed」是「出賣、洩露秘密」的意思，不是一般情感上的背叛。
generatedExample: He was accused of betraying his friends' trust.

explanationZhTw: 「screw out of someone」是口語用法，指用逼問、施壓或折磨把資訊榨出來。
generatedExample: The detectives tried to screw the truth out of the suspect.

explanationZhTw: 「torture」指酷刑、拷問。
generatedExample: He would not speak, even under torture.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「confessed in the most trivial detail」表示連最瑣碎的細節都坦白交代了，語氣強調徹底和無保留。
generatedExample: Under pressure, he confessed in the most trivial detail.

explanationZhTw: 「soon」表示「不久、很快」，這裡是在問距離被處決還要多久。
generatedExample: If the train is delayed, I hope it will arrive soon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「shoot me」不是單純描述開槍，而是指「把我槍決／處決」的語境用法。
generatedExample: The prisoner thought they might shoot him at dawn.

explanationZhTw: `give up` 是片語動詞，意思是「放棄、停止嘗試」。這裡是勸對方不要失去希望。
generatedExample: Don't give up after one mistake; keep practicing.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `cured` 是語境中的特殊用法，不是一般醫學上的「治癒」，而是指某人被「改造、矯正」到符合某種想法或立場。
generatedExample: In that story, the hero was said to be cured of his doubts.

explanationZhTw: `sooner or later` 是慣用語，意思是「遲早、早晚都會」。
generatedExample: Sooner or later, we need to decide which plan to use.
